title: bignoniaceae
intro: The bignoniaceae rank, also known as the Bignoniaceae family, is a group of flowering plants that includes approximately 800 species of trees, shrubs, and vines. These plants are commonly known as trumpet vines, cat's claws, or bottlebrush trees, and are characterized by their showy, trumpet-shaped flowers and often woody stems. The Bignoniaceae family is found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world, with the highest diversity of species in the Americas. Many species within this family are valued for their ornamental flowers and timber.
